intel_overview.headline_zh: UPC诉讼激增，英国主导FRAND裁决
intel_overview.headline_en: UPC Litigation Surges as UK Leads FRAND Rulings
intel_overview.summary_zh: 2026年上半年欧洲IP战场呈现司法活跃与制度扩容并行的态势。统一专利法院（UPC）案件量显著增长，米兰与巴黎分部密集作出撤销或维持专利效力的判决，确立严格审查标准；同时，PMAC调解机制上线，提供多元化纠纷解决路径。在标准必要专利领域，跨国巨头纷纷撤回欧陆诉讼，转向英国高等法院寻求全球FRAND费率裁定，凸显英国作为核心管辖地的地位。此外，欧盟法院在商标声誉评估及版权公平报酬方面作出关键指引，EPO则通过吸纳摩尔多瓦及推广DOCX提交进一步优化流程。整体而言，欧洲IP环境正趋向高效化、集中化与合规精细化。
top_signals: UPC进入判例法快速积累期，多地分部频繁作出实质性无效或侵权裁决，司法确定性增强但败诉风险同步上升。
英国高等法院成为SEP/FRAND全球费率争议的核心场域，主要权利人策略性撤出欧陆以聚焦英国程序。
欧盟法院强化对商标声誉全局评估及在线内容公平报酬的监管，要求企业重新审视品牌叙事与授权合规。
EPO体系持续扩容（摩尔多瓦加入第40国）并推进行政数字化（DOCX全面开放），降低申请门槛与操作成本。
cross_currents: 诉讼策略从‘多点开花’转向‘单点突破’：企业在面对UPC高昂诉讼成本时，更倾向于利用英国法院的全球裁定效力解决多国许可纠纷。
行政效率与司法严谨性双轨提升：EPO通过模块化考试和趋同实践提高授权质量，而UPC通过新系统和高结案率证明其处理复杂跨国纠纷的能力。
合规边界向非技术因素延伸：从虚构历史年份构成欺骗到LGBTI+价值观审查，IP维权不再仅关注技术或标识本身，更需符合欧盟社会价值观与反歧视框架。
implications: 针对UPC诉讼，中国企业需建立更严格的专利有效性预审机制，鉴于米兰等分部的高撤销率，应提前准备强有力的现有技术抗辩或考虑利用PMAC进行早期调解。
在SEP许可谈判中，应将英国法院的临时费率裁定作为核心参考基准，统筹管理在德国、荷兰等地的平行诉讼，避免资源分散并争取有利的全球许可条件。
品牌出海需彻底清理营销素材中的‘虚假历史’表述，并建立基于GDPR和非歧视原则的内容审核流程，以防因违反欧盟基本权利宪章而导致商标无效或面临巨额罚款。
watchlist: 英国高等法院在华为TP-Link案中对Wi-Fi 6 SEP全球费率的最终裁定及其对后续类似案件的标杆效应。
EPO第三轮实践趋同周期关于分案申请与互联网披露的具体准则落地情况，及其对专利申请策略的影响。
成员国将CJEU关于新闻出版者公平报酬的判决转化为国内法的具体时间表及强制谈判义务的实施细则。

daily_report.summary_zh: 过去48小时，欧洲知识产权动态聚焦于程序优化与实体规则收紧。UPC发布2025回顾并确认2026年设立调解仲裁中心，推动争议解决多元化；华为撤回德/UPC诉请，将FRAND战场集中于英国高院，凸显管辖权博弈。实体法方面，EPO明确“注册启用”为非技术特征，CJEU裁定忽视入住率的固定版权费率构成滥用支配地位，德国警示AI恶意抢注风险。企业需同步调整诉讼策略与合规审查重点。

storylines.title_en: Huawei Withdraws UPC Claims, Focuses FRAND Dispute on UK
storylines.what_happened_en: Huawei withdrew infringement suits against TP-Link at the UPC and Munich Regional Court, focusing resources on UK High Court FRAND proceedings following interim rate determinations.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: This shift highlights the strategic use of UK courts for efficient SEP rate setting, influencing global licensing benchmarks and requiring coordinated multi-jurisdictional defense strategies.

storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 标志着欧洲专利争议解决向高效、保密的替代性纠纷解决机制（ADR）倾斜，降低维权成本。

storylines.what_happened_en: The EPO classified registration-based enablement as non-technical, while the CJEU ruled that flat copyright fees ignoring occupancy rates may constitute an abuse of dominant position.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: These rulings clarify technical contribution requirements for patents and enforce proportionality in copyright licensing, impacting innovation strategies and royalty negotiation frameworks across Europe.

storylines.title_en: UK Appeal Court Clarifies Online Sales Restrictions
storylines.what_happened_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled that prohibiting authorized retailers from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law within selective distribution systems.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition laws, avoiding blanket bans or price-fixing arrangements.

report_items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
report_items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Registration-Based Enablement Non-Technical in IoT Smoking System Case
report_items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
report_items.summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
report_items.summary_en: This case concerns patent proceedings for Philip Morris’ electrically heated smoking system. The Technical Board of Appeal determined that the feature requiring device enablement only after registration via an Internet host constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, this requirement does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a registration mandate causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical. This decision establishes that fu...
core_points_zh: 注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性
实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质
在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.
Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.
Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
report_items.insight_en: For applicants in the IoT and smart hardware sectors, relying on pure commercial logic, such as paid unlocks or registration activation, as core technical features risks rejection for lack of technical contribution. Drafting strategies should instead focus on specific technical improvements enabling these functions, such as optimized communication protocols or security encryption algorithms. Claims must address concr...
insight_points_zh: 避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利
聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.
Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.
What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases.

report_items.title_zh: UPC官方2025年度回顾：五大亮点解析
report_items.title_en: UPC 2025 Annual Review: Five Key Highlights for Patent Litigants and Rights Holders
report_items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025
report_items.summary_zh: Bristows律所发布分析，解读统一专利法院（UPC）发布的2025年官方年度回顾报告。该报告是UPC的第二份年度报告，全面梳理了2025年的机构活动、案件趋势及运营发展。文章从中提炼出五个对UPC用户至关重要的亮点，旨在帮助利益相关者更好地理解UPC的最新动态与司法实践走向，为在欧专利诉讼策略提供参考依据。
report_items.summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, offering a comprehensive overview of institutional activities, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. This report serves as a critical resource for understanding the court's evolving jurisprudence and procedural efficiency. Bristows analyzes the document to extract five significant highlights that are particularly relevant for UPC users. These insights provide legal practitioners and corporate counsel with essential data to navigate the complex landscape of European patent litigation. By examini...
core_points_zh: UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势
报告详细分析了UPC的运营发展状况，为法律从业者提供关键数据支持
文章提炼五大核心亮点，聚焦对UPC用户最具参考价值的实务动态
core_points_en: The UPC’s second annual review comprehensively details institutional activities and emerging case trends observed during 2025.
Operational developments and statistical data are analyzed to provide legal practitioners with crucial support for case management.
Five core highlights are extracted to focus on practical dynamics offering the most value to current and prospective UPC users.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor the case trends and procedural shifts outlined in the UPC’s annual review. Particular attention is warranted regarding judicial practices concerning preliminary injunctions and jurisdictional matters. It is advisable to proactively assess patent portfolio risks within Europe and optimize litigation response strategies. Leveraging these insights allows comp...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UPC case trends to optimize European patent litigation and compliance response strategies.
Practice note: Utilize annual review insights to anticipate judicial practice, proactively avoid infringement risks, and protect rights.
What to monitor: Developments in preliminary injunction standards and jurisdictional rulings affecting cross-border enforcement.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
report_items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on Whether Pro-Drugs Qualify for Independent SPC Protection
report_items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
report_items.summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has accepted a referral in case C-794/25, seeking clarification on whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient can independently obtain Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The dispute centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug marketed by Takeda, and its parent compound dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX received marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to distinct safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain regarding its status under EU regulations. Specifically, it is...
core_points_zh: CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护
争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质
裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.
The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.
The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor this precedent, as the outcome significantly impacts SPC eligibility for pro-drug pipelines in Europe. If pro-drugs are deemed ineligible for independent protection, companies may face reduced market exclusivity periods. Consequently, stakeholders should evaluate alternative strategies to extend commercial advantages, such as optimizing patent portfolios or leveraging other regul...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith Trademark Filings in Germany: Rising Risks and Strategic Implications
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
report_items.summary_en: This analysis examines the application of 'Bösgläubigkeit' (bad faith) within German trademark law. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad-faith applications since mid-2025, often targeting unregistered signs identified via AI tools to exert pressure through online platforms. Under the German Trademark Act, applications lacking genuine use intent and aimed at obstructing competition or securing undue advantages are subject to refusal or invalidation. Determining bad faith requires a comprehensive assessment combining subjective intent with objective circumstances. This...
core_points_zh: 德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击
德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效
恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响
core_points_en: The DPMA warns of increased bad-faith filings using AI to target unregistered signs for strategic blocking purposes.
German law allows refusal or invalidation of registrations lacking genuine use intent and aiming to abuse rights.
Bad faith determination combines subjective intent with objective context, heavily influenced by EU judicial precedents.
report_items.insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants should secure early trademark registration in Germany to establish robust rights bases against potential squatting. Implementing active monitoring systems is crucial to detect potentially bad-faith filings that could lead to rapid product removals on e-commerce platforms. If facing such allegations or actions, rights holders must diligently collect evidence of prior use. Leveraging German statutor...
insight_points_zh: 尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险
遭遇恶意指控时，应积极收集在先使用证据，依法提起无效宣告以维权
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure early German registration and monitor filings to prevent bad-faith squatting and e-commerce takedowns.
Practice note: Collect prior use evidence promptly to support invalidation actions against abusive trademark registrations.
What to monitor: Track DPMA notices and EU case law developments regarding bad faith definitions and enforcement trends.

report_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
report_items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Legality of Online Restrictions in Selective Distribution
report_items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
report_items.summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in Deckers UK Limited v Up & Running (UK) Limited that preventing an authorised retailer from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law. This decision overturns the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s earlier judgment, confirming that restrictions within selective distribution systems require a nuanced assessment of their legal and economic context to establish sufficient harm. The ruling aligns with established case law such as Coty, emphasizing that online sales limitations are not presumed illegal. However, this does not sign...
core_points_zh: 在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度
上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性
企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险
core_points_en: Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.
The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.
Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating selective distribution systems in Europe must carefully distinguish between permissible qualitative criteria and prohibited hardcore restrictions. While brands retain discretion to protect their image by restricting sales on unbranded discount platforms, they must document objective justifications linked to brand positioning or preventing free-riding. Blanket bans on online sales or any...
insight_points_zh: 制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售
严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.
Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets.

report_items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
report_items.title_en: Strategic IP Mediation: Leveraging EUIPO and UPC Mechanisms for Efficient Dispute Resolution
report_items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
report_items.summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
report_items.summary_en: Mediation has emerged as a preferred method for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering efficiency, confidentiality, and commercially oriented solutions. Data from the World Intellectual Property Organization indicates a significant 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises representing a substantial portion of participants. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Office has expanded its mediation services to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court is set to inaugura...
core_points_zh: 调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高
EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心
调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案
core_points_en: WIPO reported a 42% surge in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising the majority of involved parties.
The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.
Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should strategically consider alternative dispute resolution mechanisms to manage IP risks effectively. The expansion of EUIPO mediation services and the upcoming UPC Patent Mediation Centre provide accessible, cost-effective forums for resolving conflicts without the adversarial nature of court proceedings. This approach is particularly advantageous for protecting trade secret...
insight_points_zh: 优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险
建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.
Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers.

report_items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
report_items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act: Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examinations
report_items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
report_items.summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, marking a significant shift in the national intellectual property framework. The reform introduces a mandatory prior-art search for all applications and establishes an optional full substantive examination procedure. Additionally, the updated law accepts technical documents in English and implements a new appeal system. These changes transition Switzerland from a purely formal examination model to a stricter review regime. The primary objectives are to enhance overall patent quality and increase legal certai...
core_points_zh: 瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案
引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制
接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统
core_points_en: The Swiss Federal Assembly approved a partial Patent Act revision in March 2024, introducing major procedural changes.
New rules mandate prior-art searches and offer applicants the option to request full substantive examination.
The reform accepts English-language technical documents and establishes a completely new patent appeal system.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants filing in Switzerland must adapt to the new mandatory prior-art search requirements, which may increase initial costs and processing times. Applicants should optimize early-stage prior-art analysis and budget planning to accommodate these changes. The acceptance of English technical documents offers an opportunity to reduce translation expenses, provided filings are managed efficiently. Furthermor...
insight_points_zh: 适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划
善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to mandatory prior-art search requirements by optimizing early-stage analysis and budget planning.
Practice note: Leverage the acceptance of English technical documents to reduce translation costs while ensuring compliance.
What to monitor: Assess the commercial necessity of optional substantive examination against higher authorization thresholds.

report_items.title_zh: 华为撤回UPC及德国侵权诉请，FRAND战聚焦英国高院
report_items.title_en: Huawei Withdraws UPC and German Claims, Focusing FRAND Dispute on UK High Court
report_items.title: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
report_items.summary_zh: 华为已撤回针对TP-Link在统一专利法院（UPC）和慕尼黑地方法院的侵权指控。随着英国高等法院法官Richard Meade确定临时许可费率，双方关于Wi-Fi 6技术的争议焦点完全转向英国的FRAND费率设定程序。此前华为于2025年夏季在德国发起诉讼，目前该行动被视为配合英国费率裁定的逻辑步骤，同时华为在中国法院的诉讼仍在进行。
report_items.summary_en: Huawei has formally withdrawn its patent infringement claims against TP-Link at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. This strategic move follows the UK High Court’s recent determination of an interim licence rate by Judge Richard Meade. Consequently, the dispute concerning Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents is now primarily centered on the FRAND rate-setting proceedings in London. Huawei originally initiated litigation in Germany during summer 2025, filing cases at both the UPC local division in Munich and the regional court. While the European continental actions have...
core_points_zh: 华为撤回UPC及德国侵权诉请，集中资源应对英国FRAND费率程序
英国高等法院已裁定临时许可费率，成为当前争议解决的核心场域
案件涉及Wi-Fi 6标准必要专利，体现跨国SEP诉讼的策略协同
core_points_en: Huawei withdrew infringement suits at the UPC and Munich Regional Court to concentrate resources on UK FRAND proceedings.
The UK High Court's interim licence rate ruling establishes London as the central venue for resolving this SEP dispute.
The case involves Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents, demonstrating coordinated cross-border litigation strategies among SEP holders.
report_items.insight_zh: 此动态表明SEP权利人正利用英国法院的高效费率裁定优势，通过撤回其他法域诉讼施压。中国出口企业应重视英国FRAND判例对全球许可费率的锚定效应，积极应诉并争取有利费率；同时需统筹中、欧多地诉讼策略，避免被动接受单方裁定，善用司法管辖权博弈维护自身权益。
report_items.insight_en: This development highlights the increasing strategic reliance on the UK High Court for determining global FRAND rates, leveraging its efficiency and precedential weight. Rights holders are increasingly using withdrawals in other jurisdictions to streamline litigation and pressure implementers into accepting UK-determined benchmarks. For market participants, particularly those with significant export exposure, underst...
insight_points_zh: 关注英国FRAND裁定对全球许可费率的标杆影响
统筹多国诉讼策略以应对权利人的管辖权选择
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how UK FRAND rulings influence global licensing benchmarks and impact royalty obligations for Wi-Fi 6 technologies.
Practice note: Coordinate multi-jurisdictional litigation strategies to counterbalance rights holder forum selection and avoid passive acceptance of adverse terms.
What to monitor: Track the progression of parallel Chinese proceedings and their potential interaction with the final UK FRAND determination.

report_items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
report_items.title_en: Revised EU TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Patent Pools, FRAND, and Collective Negotiations
report_items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
report_items.summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. This new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the established safe harbour structure under Article 101(3) TFEU for agreements meeting market share thresholds and avoiding hardcore restrictions. Significantly, the revised Guidelines provide detailed competition law guidance for modern licensing structures, including technology pools, standard-essential technology (SEP) licensing, data licensing, and licensing negotiatio...
core_points_zh: 修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架
新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导
企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护
core_points_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.
New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.
Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: Stakeholders involved in technology licensing should closely monitor the updated compliance requirements for SEP licensing and patent pool participation, particularly regarding collective negotiation mechanisms. It is advisable to review existing licensing agreements to ensure they do not contain hardcore restrictions that could jeopardize block exemption status. Furthermore, parties should utilize the new Guidelines...
insight_points_zh: 重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求
利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust compliance requirements under the revised TTBER.
Practice note: Leverage updated guidelines to optimize participation in patent pools and structure collective negotiation strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU：忽视入住率的版权费率过高或构成滥用市场支配地位
report_items.title_en: CJEU Rules Ignoring Hotel Occupancy in Copyright Fees May Abuse Dominant Position
report_items.title: Copyright royalty rates excessive of economic value may be abusive of dominant position, rules CJEU against Czech CMO
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟法院裁定捷克集体管理组织OSA向酒店收取固定版权费未考虑房间入住率，可能违反TFEU第102条构成滥用支配地位。法院强调版权许可费应与被许可人实际获得的经济价值成比例，若行政上可行，忽略入住率等实际使用情况的定价属不公平高价。该裁决要求版权收费机制必须反映真实经济价值，对欧盟内版权许可实践具有深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU ruled that a Czech collective management organisation’s practice of charging flat-rate copyright fees without considering hotel room occupancy may constitute an abuse of dominant position under Article 102 TFEU. The Court emphasized that royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value received by licensees and the specific use of protected works. Ignoring factual usage indicators, such as occupancy rates or seasonal closures, creates a disconnect between price and value. Where administratively feasible, pricing models must reflect these realities...
core_points_zh: CJEU裁定忽略入住率的固定版权费率可能构成滥用市场支配地位
版权许可费必须与被许可人实际获得的经济价值及作品使用情况成比例
若行政可行，集体管理组织需采用基于实际使用（如入住率）的计费方法
core_points_en: Flat-rate copyright fees ignoring hotel occupancy may abuse dominant position under EU competition law.
Royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value and specific use of protected works.
Collective management organisations should adopt usage-based billing methods where administratively feasible.
report_items.insight_zh: 在欧运营酒店、餐饮及娱乐设施的中国企业应重新审查现有版权许可协议。若支付固定费用且未挂钩实际使用情况，可依据此判例主张费率不公并寻求调整。建议评估历史缴费记录，必要时通过谈判或法律途径争取更公平的计费方式，以降低合规成本并规避反垄断风险。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders and licensees operating in the EU should review existing copyright licensing agreements, particularly those involving fixed fees disconnected from actual usage metrics like occupancy. This CJEU precedent provides a basis to challenge unfair pricing structures that fail to reflect the real economic value derived from protected works. Stakeholders may leverage this ruling during negotiations to secure mo...
insight_points_zh: 在欧中企应审查版权许可费是否与实际使用情况挂钩以规避反垄断风险
可依据CJEU判例主张固定费率不公，通过谈判或诉讼争取合理计费方式
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Licensees can challenge fixed fees lacking usage links to mitigate antitrust risks.
Practice note: Use CJEU precedent to negotiate fairer, usage-based copyright royalty adjustments.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding disproportionate royalties by collective management organisations.

quick_takeaways: 华为撤回UPC及德国侵权诉请，FRAND战聚焦英...：华为已撤回针对TP-Link在统一专利法院（UPC）...
EPO与CJEU收紧授权与反垄断标准：EPO裁定注册启用属非技术特征；CJEU裁定忽视入住率的固定版权费率...
英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引：英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定...
daily_report.executive_line_en: European IP dynamics show diverging litigation strategies and rising compliance thresholds: UPC strengthens ADR, while courts tighten patent and copyright abuse standards.
daily_report.summary_en: Recent European intellectual property developments highlight a dual trend of procedural optimization and stricter substantive rules. The Unified Patent Court (UPC) released its 2025 annual review and confirmed the establishment of a mediation and arbitration center in 2026, promoting diversified dispute resolution mechanisms. In parallel, Huawei withdrew infringement claims in Germany and at the UPC against TP-Link, concentrating its Standard Essential Patent (SEP) licensing dispute on the UK High Court. This move underscores strategic jurisdictional maneuvering in global FRAND negotiations. On substantive law fronts, the European Patent Office (EPO) ruled that registration-based device enablement constitutes a non-technical business constraint, tightening patentability criteria for IoT systems. Meanwhile, the Court of Justice of the EU (CJEU) determined that fixed copyright fees ignoring actual usage metrics may abuse dominant market positions. Additionally, German authorities warned of increased AI-driven bad-faith trademark filings. Rights holders must simultaneously adjust cross-border litigation strategies and enhance compliance reviews regarding licensing structures and brand...
what_changed_vs_last_window_en: UPC formally confirmed the 2026 launch of its mediation and arbitration center, advancing alternative dispute resolution mechanisms.
Huawei strategically shifted its European SEP litigation focus from multi-jurisdictional parallel suits to concentrated UK FRAND proceedings.
EPO issued new guidance clarifying the boundary between commercial logic and technical features, raising patent authorization thresholds.
next_watch_72h_en: Monitor subsequent developments regarding interim license rates in the Huawei UK High Court case.
Track specific enforcement cases by the German DPMA concerning AI-driven bad-faith trademark squatting.
Observe initial guidance cases from the European Commission on patent pool compliance under the revised TTBER.
key_moves_en: Huawei strategically withdraws partial European claims to concentrate efforts on securing favorable UK FRAND rates.
EPO excludes pure commercial logic from technical contributions, increasing patent grant strictness.
CJEU establishes principles linking copyright fees to actual usage to prevent abusive pricing practices.
business_actions_en: Review SEP licensing agreements to ensure antitrust compliance with the revised TTBER and leverage safe harbor provisions.
Complete early trademark registrations in key markets like Germany and establish monitoring systems to counter AI-driven bad-faith filings.
Incorporate pre-agreed mediation clauses into contracts to prioritize EUIPO and UPC mediation mechanisms for cost-effective cross-border dispute resolution.
watchlist_en: Progress of the CJEU ruling on whether pro-drugs qualify for independent Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection.
Implementation details of the substantive examination system under Switzerland's new Patent Act.
Application of the UK Court of Appeal’s selective distribution guidelines in e-commerce contexts.
quick_takeaways_en: UK courts remain pivotal for determining global FRAND benchmarks in SEP disputes.
Technical patent claims must avoid reliance on purely administrative or commercial constraints.
Copyright royalty structures must demonstrate proportionality to actual economic value and usage metrics.
regional_shift_en: Increased reliance on UK jurisdiction for SEP rate-setting
Stricter technical character assessment in EPO practice
strategic_implications_en: Litigants should prioritize forum selection based on procedural efficiency and rate-setting precedents.
Patent drafting must emphasize specific technical implementations over business method logic.
Licensing frameworks require regular audits to align with evolving competition law standards.

weekly_report.headline_zh: 欧盟许可新规生效，UPC与CJEU强化合规边界
weekly_report.headline_en: EU Licensing Rules Tighten; UPC/CJEU Set Compliance Boundaries
weekly_report.executive_line: 本周修订版TTBER正式生效重塑技术许可反垄断框架；UPC收紧临时禁令举证标准，CJEU明确版权费率滥用红线，欧洲IP合规与诉讼门槛显著抬升。
weekly_report.summary_zh: 2026年5月17-23日，欧洲知识产权环境呈现“规则细化”与“司法从严”双重趋势。修订版TTBER及指南生效，为专利池和SEP许可提供明确竞争法安全港。司法层面，UPC杜塞尔多夫分院严格限定方法专利侵权比对需基于单一产品，提高临时禁令获取难度；CJEU裁定忽视实际使用率的固定版权费率构成市场支配地位滥用。同时，瑞士引入专利实质审查，英国成为FRAND争议新焦点。企业需立即调整许可协议结构，优化跨境诉讼策略以应对更高的合规与举证要求。

storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 显著增加权利人获取临时禁令的难度，仅凭营销材料或推测性证言不再足以确立侵权事实。

weekly_report.weekly_thesis: 本周欧洲IP生态从“立法扩张”转向“司法与合规的精细化治理”，权利行使边界被进一步厘清。
rising_themes: 技术许可的反垄断合规（TTBER）
UPC专利诉讼的举证标准化

regional_shift: FRAND争议解决重心从大陆法系国家向英国普通法系倾斜
瑞士专利制度向欧洲主流实质审查体系靠拢
strategic_implications: 企业在欧技术授权需建立动态合规审查机制，以应对反垄断监管升级
跨境IP维权需更注重证据的单一性与直接性，降低对间接证据的依赖
版权成本管控需从固定预算转向基于业务数据的动态谈判模式

report_items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
report_items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Registration-Based Enablement Non-Technical in IoT Smoking System Case
report_items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
report_items.summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
report_items.summary_en: This case concerns patent proceedings for Philip Morris’ electrically heated smoking system. The Technical Board of Appeal determined that the feature requiring device enablement only after registration via an Internet host constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, this requirement does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a registration mandate causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical. This decision establishes that fu...
core_points_zh: 注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性
实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质
在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.
Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.
Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
report_items.insight_en: For applicants in the IoT and smart hardware sectors, relying on pure commercial logic, such as paid unlocks or registration activation, as core technical features risks rejection for lack of technical contribution. Drafting strategies should instead focus on specific technical improvements enabling these functions, such as optimized communication protocols or security encryption algorithms. Claims must address concr...
insight_points_zh: 避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利
聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.
Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.
What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases.

report_items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025

report_items.summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, offering a comprehensive overview of institutional activities, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. This report serves as a critical resource for understanding the court's evolving jurisprudence and procedural efficiency. Bristows analyzes the document to extract five significant highlights that are particularly relevant for UPC users. These insights provide legal practitioners and corporate counsel with essential data to navigate the complex landscape of European patent litigation. By examini...
core_points_zh: UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势
报告详细分析了UPC的运营发展状况，为法律从业者提供关键数据支持
文章提炼五大核心亮点，聚焦对UPC用户最具参考价值的实务动态
core_points_en: The UPC’s second annual review comprehensively details institutional activities and emerging case trends observed during 2025.
Operational developments and statistical data are analyzed to provide legal practitioners with crucial support for case management.
Five core highlights are extracted to focus on practical dynamics offering the most value to current and prospective UPC users.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。

Particular attention is warranted regarding judicial practices concerning preliminary injunctions and jurisdictional matters. It is advisable to proactively assess patent portfolio risks within Europe and optimize litigation response strategies. Leveraging these insights allows comp...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UPC case trends to optimize European patent litigation and compliance response strategies.
Practice note: Utilize annual review insights to anticipate judicial practice, proactively avoid infringement risks, and protect rights.
What to monitor: Developments in preliminary injunction standards and jurisdictional rulings affecting cross-border enforcement.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
report_items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on Whether Pro-Drugs Qualify for Independent SPC Protection
report_items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
report_items.summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has accepted a referral in case C-794/25, seeking clarification on whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient can independently obtain Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The dispute centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug marketed by Takeda, and its parent compound dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX received marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to distinct safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain regarding its status under EU regulations. Specifically, it is...
core_points_zh: CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护
争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质
裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.
The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.
The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor this precedent, as the outcome significantly impacts SPC eligibility for pro-drug pipelines in Europe. If pro-drugs are deemed ineligible for independent protection, companies may face reduced market exclusivity periods. Consequently, stakeholders should evaluate alternative strategies to extend commercial advantages, such as optimizing patent portfolios or leveraging other regul...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith Trademark Filings in Germany: Rising Risks and Strategic Implications
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
report_items.summary_en: This analysis examines the application of 'Bösgläubigkeit' (bad faith) within German trademark law. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad-faith applications since mid-2025, often targeting unregistered signs identified via AI tools to exert pressure through online platforms. Under the German Trademark Act, applications lacking genuine use intent and aimed at obstructing competition or securing undue advantages are subject to refusal or invalidation. Determining bad faith requires a comprehensive assessment combining subjective intent with objective circumstances. This...
core_points_zh: 德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击
德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效
恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响
core_points_en: The DPMA warns of increased bad-faith filings using AI to target unregistered signs for strategic blocking purposes.
German law allows refusal or invalidation of registrations lacking genuine use intent and aiming to abuse rights.
Bad faith determination combines subjective intent with objective context, heavily influenced by EU judicial precedents.
report_items.insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants should secure early trademark registration in Germany to establish robust rights bases against potential squatting. Implementing active monitoring systems is crucial to detect potentially bad-faith filings that could lead to rapid product removals on e-commerce platforms. If facing such allegations or actions, rights holders must diligently collect evidence of prior use. Leveraging German statutor...
insight_points_zh: 尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险
遭遇恶意指控时，应积极收集在先使用证据，依法提起无效宣告以维权
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure early German registration and monitor filings to prevent bad-faith squatting and e-commerce takedowns.
Practice note: Collect prior use evidence promptly to support invalidation actions against abusive trademark registrations.
What to monitor: Track DPMA notices and EU case law developments regarding bad faith definitions and enforcement trends.

report_items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
report_items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Preliminary Injunction: Strict Single-Product Infringement Proof Required
report_items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
report_items.summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
report_items.summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed an applicant’s request for preliminary injunctions on May 12, 2026. The Court emphasized that applicants must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not. Regarding clear aligner patents, the ruling established that all technical features of a method claim must be embodied within a single product. Consequently, different treatment phases executed by separate aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Since the applicant relied exclusively on promotional materials without providing specific technical...
core_points_zh: 申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性
方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合
仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实
core_points_en: Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC’s strict interpretation of 'single product' requirements for multi-stage medical treatments like clear aligner therapy. Parties seeking or defending against preliminary injunctions must prepare detailed, feature-by-feature technical comparisons rather than relying on marketing collateral. For companies operating in Europe, this highlights the necessity of conducting thorough Freedom-...
insight_points_zh: 重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险
完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.
Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.
What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims.

report_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
report_items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Legality of Online Restrictions in Selective Distribution
report_items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
report_items.summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in Deckers UK Limited v Up & Running (UK) Limited that preventing an authorised retailer from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law. This decision overturns the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s earlier judgment, confirming that restrictions within selective distribution systems require a nuanced assessment of their legal and economic context to establish sufficient harm. The ruling aligns with established case law such as Coty, emphasizing that online sales limitations are not presumed illegal. However, this does not sign...
core_points_zh: 在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度
上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性
企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险
core_points_en: Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.
The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.
Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating selective distribution systems in Europe must carefully distinguish between permissible qualitative criteria and prohibited hardcore restrictions. While brands retain discretion to protect their image by restricting sales on unbranded discount platforms, they must document objective justifications linked to brand positioning or preventing free-riding. Blanket bans on online sales or any...
insight_points_zh: 制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售
严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.
Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets.

report_items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
report_items.title_en: Strategic IP Mediation: Leveraging EUIPO and UPC Mechanisms for Efficient Dispute Resolution
report_items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
report_items.summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
report_items.summary_en: Mediation has emerged as a preferred method for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering efficiency, confidentiality, and commercially oriented solutions. Data from the World Intellectual Property Organization indicates a significant 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises representing a substantial portion of participants. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Office has expanded its mediation services to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court is set to inaugura...
core_points_zh: 调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高
EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心
调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案
core_points_en: WIPO reported a 42% surge in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising the majority of involved parties.
The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.
Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should strategically consider alternative dispute resolution mechanisms to manage IP risks effectively. The expansion of EUIPO mediation services and the upcoming UPC Patent Mediation Centre provide accessible, cost-effective forums for resolving conflicts without the adversarial nature of court proceedings. This approach is particularly advantageous for protecting trade secret...
insight_points_zh: 优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险
建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.
Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers.

report_items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
report_items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act: Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examinations
report_items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
report_items.summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, marking a significant shift in the national intellectual property framework. The reform introduces a mandatory prior-art search for all applications and establishes an optional full substantive examination procedure. Additionally, the updated law accepts technical documents in English and implements a new appeal system. These changes transition Switzerland from a purely formal examination model to a stricter review regime. The primary objectives are to enhance overall patent quality and increase legal certai...
core_points_zh: 瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案
引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制
接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统
core_points_en: The Swiss Federal Assembly approved a partial Patent Act revision in March 2024, introducing major procedural changes.
New rules mandate prior-art searches and offer applicants the option to request full substantive examination.
The reform accepts English-language technical documents and establishes a completely new patent appeal system.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants filing in Switzerland must adapt to the new mandatory prior-art search requirements, which may increase initial costs and processing times. Applicants should optimize early-stage prior-art analysis and budget planning to accommodate these changes. The acceptance of English technical documents offers an opportunity to reduce translation expenses, provided filings are managed efficiently. Furthermor...
insight_points_zh: 适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划
善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to mandatory prior-art search requirements by optimizing early-stage analysis and budget planning.
Practice note: Leverage the acceptance of English technical documents to reduce translation costs while ensuring compliance.
What to monitor: Assess the commercial necessity of optional substantive examination against higher authorization thresholds.

report_items.title_zh: 华为撤回UPC及德国侵权诉请，FRAND战聚焦英国高院
report_items.title_en: Huawei Withdraws UPC and German Claims, Focusing FRAND Dispute on UK High Court
report_items.title: FRAND battle between Huawei and TP-Link now focused at UK High Court
report_items.summary_zh: 华为已撤回针对TP-Link在统一专利法院（UPC）和慕尼黑地方法院的侵权指控。随着英国高等法院法官Richard Meade确定临时许可费率，双方关于Wi-Fi 6技术的争议焦点完全转向英国的FRAND费率设定程序。此前华为于2025年夏季在德国发起诉讼，目前该行动被视为配合英国费率裁定的逻辑步骤，同时华为在中国法院的诉讼仍在进行。
report_items.summary_en: Huawei has formally withdrawn its patent infringement claims against TP-Link at the Unified Patent Court (UPC) and the Munich Regional Court. This strategic move follows the UK High Court’s recent determination of an interim licence rate by Judge Richard Meade. Consequently, the dispute concerning Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents is now primarily centered on the FRAND rate-setting proceedings in London. Huawei originally initiated litigation in Germany during summer 2025, filing cases at both the UPC local division in Munich and the regional court. While the European continental actions have...
core_points_zh: 华为撤回UPC及德国侵权诉请，集中资源应对英国FRAND费率程序
英国高等法院已裁定临时许可费率，成为当前争议解决的核心场域
案件涉及Wi-Fi 6标准必要专利，体现跨国SEP诉讼的策略协同
core_points_en: Huawei withdrew infringement suits at the UPC and Munich Regional Court to concentrate resources on UK FRAND proceedings.
The UK High Court's interim licence rate ruling establishes London as the central venue for resolving this SEP dispute.
The case involves Wi-Fi 6 standard essential patents, demonstrating coordinated cross-border litigation strategies among SEP holders.
report_items.insight_zh: 此动态表明SEP权利人正利用英国法院的高效费率裁定优势，通过撤回其他法域诉讼施压。中国出口企业应重视英国FRAND判例对全球许可费率的锚定效应，积极应诉并争取有利费率；同时需统筹中、欧多地诉讼策略，避免被动接受单方裁定，善用司法管辖权博弈维护自身权益。
report_items.insight_en: This development highlights the increasing strategic reliance on the UK High Court for determining global FRAND rates, leveraging its efficiency and precedential weight. Rights holders are increasingly using withdrawals in other jurisdictions to streamline litigation and pressure implementers into accepting UK-determined benchmarks. For market participants, particularly those with significant export exposure, underst...
insight_points_zh: 关注英国FRAND裁定对全球许可费率的标杆影响
统筹多国诉讼策略以应对权利人的管辖权选择
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor how UK FRAND rulings influence global licensing benchmarks and impact royalty obligations for Wi-Fi 6 technologies.
Practice note: Coordinate multi-jurisdictional litigation strategies to counterbalance rights holder forum selection and avoid passive acceptance of adverse terms.
What to monitor: Track the progression of parallel Chinese proceedings and their potential interaction with the final UK FRAND determination.

report_items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
report_items.title_en: Revised EU TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Patent Pools, FRAND, and Collective Negotiations
report_items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
report_items.summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. This new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the established safe harbour structure under Article 101(3) TFEU for agreements meeting market share thresholds and avoiding hardcore restrictions. Significantly, the revised Guidelines provide detailed competition law guidance for modern licensing structures, including technology pools, standard-essential technology (SEP) licensing, data licensing, and licensing negotiatio...
core_points_zh: 修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架
新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导
企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护
core_points_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.
New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.
Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: Stakeholders involved in technology licensing should closely monitor the updated compliance requirements for SEP licensing and patent pool participation, particularly regarding collective negotiation mechanisms. It is advisable to review existing licensing agreements to ensure they do not contain hardcore restrictions that could jeopardize block exemption status. Furthermore, parties should utilize the new Guidelines...
insight_points_zh: 重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求
利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust compliance requirements under the revised TTBER.
Practice note: Leverage updated guidelines to optimize participation in patent pools and structure collective negotiation strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements.

daily_audio_latest.headline_zh: UPC调解中心落地，华为撤诉转战英国FRAND
daily_audio_latest.headline_en: UPC Mediation Center Launches; Huawei Shifts FRAND Battle to UK
daily_audio_latest.summary_zh: 过去48小时，欧洲知识产权动态聚焦于程序优化与实体规则收紧。UPC发布2025回顾并确认2026年设立调解仲裁中心，推动争议解决多元化；华为撤回德/UPC诉请，将FRAND战场集中于英国高院，凸显管辖权博弈。实体法方面，EPO明确“注册启用”为非技术特征，CJEU裁定忽视入住率的固定版权费率构成滥用支配地位，德国警示AI恶意抢注风险。企业需同步调整诉讼策略与合规审查重点。
daily_audio_latest.summary_en: Recent European intellectual property developments highlight a dual trend of procedural optimization and stricter substantive rules. The Unified Patent Court (UPC) released its 2025 annual review and confirmed the establishment of a mediation and arbitration center in 2026, promoting diversified dispute resolution mechanisms. In parallel, Huawei withdrew infringement claims in Germany and at the UPC against TP-Link, concentrating its Standard Essential Patent (SEP) licensing dispute on the UK High Court. This move underscores strategic jurisdictional maneuvering in global FRAND negotiations. On substantive law fronts, the European Patent Office (EPO) ruled that registration-based device enablement constitutes a non-technical business constraint, tightening patentability criteria for IoT systems. Meanwhile, the Court of Justice of the EU (CJEU) determined that fixed copyright fees ignoring actual usage metrics may abuse dominant market positions. Additionally, German authorities warned of increased AI-driven bad-faith trademark filings. Rights holders must simultaneously adjust cross-border litigation strategies and enhance compliance reviews regarding licensing structures and brand...

transcript_lines_zh: 今天是2026年5月23日。以下是今天欧洲 IP 圈最重要的几条变化。
先看第一条，注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性。值得关注的是，避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利。
再看第二条，UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势。后续要看欧洲层面会不会出现进一步官方动作或配套安排。
再看第三条，CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护。后续要看欧洲层面会不会出现进一步官方动作或配套安排。
最后看第四条，德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击。值得关注的是，尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险。
总结来看，今天的欧洲 IP 动态仍然围绕专利、商标、SEP标准必要专利展开，接下来值得继续关注CJEU关于前药SPC资格案的裁决进程。
transcript_lines_en: Today is May 23, 2026. Here are the most relevant European IP developments in today's briefing.

First, Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character. Why it matters: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.

Next, The UPC’s second annual review comprehensively details institutional activities and emerging case trends observed during. Why it matters: Monitor UPC case trends to optimize European patent litigation and compliance response strategies.

Next, The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredien. Why it matters: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.

Finally, The DPMA warns of increased bad-faith filings using AI to target unregistered signs for strategic blocking purposes. Why it matters: Secure early German registration and monitor filings to prevent bad-faith squatting and e-commerce takedowns.

Overall, today's European IP signals center on Patents, Trade marks, General IP. The next point to watch is Progress of the CJEU ruling on whether pro-drugs qualify for independent Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) prot....

topics.headline_en: UPC Case Law Consolidation and PMAC Alternative Dispute Resolution Launch
topics.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，UPC通过年报及多项裁决确立司法权威。赫尔辛基、杜塞尔多夫等分院在等同原则、权利要求解释上形成稳定判例，强调单一产品侵权比对及严格程序合规。同时，PMAC调解仲裁规则获批并上线系统，提供替代性争议解决新路径。企业需警惕功能性限定带来的零部件直接侵权风险，并利用ADR机制降低维权成本。
key_points: 司法实践趋稳：各分院在等同侵权判定、权利要求解释（特别是功能性限定）及临时禁令举证责任上形成统一且严格的裁判标准，强调技术特征必须在单一产品中实现。
程序合规高压：法院严守上诉期限、证据提交时限及费用担保要求，虽对“可原谅错误”留有救济空间，但技术性违规导致失权风险极高，诉讼策略需极度严谨。
ADR机制激活：PMAC程序规则获批且案件管理系统上线，为SEP/FRAND纠纷及普通专利争议提供官方认可的调解与仲裁渠道，成为降低在欧诉讼成本的新选项。
actions: 优化专利布局与FTO排查：针对出口欧洲的硬件组件，重点审查是否触犯“功能性限定”或“单独部件销售即侵权”的判例红线，避免仅因营销材料或跨阶段组合被诉。
建立严密的诉讼响应SOP：设立双重期限核查机制以应对UPC严格的程序时限；收到侵权通知后，立即书面表达许可意愿以保留FRAND抗辩权，并谨慎评估官网声明的法律后果。
评估PMAC调解可行性：对于高成本的SEP/FRAND纠纷或事实清晰的侵权案，主动利用PMAC系统进行早期调解或仲裁，以规避高昂的主诉讼费用及强制公示判决的品牌声誉风险。
watchlist: 观察主要分部（如杜塞尔多夫、慕尼黑）早期判例是否进一步固化特定的法律教义，以及其对其他成员国法院的辐射影响。
关注PMAC在实际运行中的案件受理量、调解成功率及费用结构，评估其作为主流争议解决手段的实际效能。
监测UPC关于暂停执行临时禁令及保证金要求的裁量标准变化，特别是针对中小企业财务困难认定的具体门槛。
topics.summary_en: During the first half of 2026, the Unified Patent Court solidified its judicial authority through its annual report and significant rulings. Local Divisions in Helsinki and Düsseldorf established consistent precedents regarding the doctrine of equivalents and claim construction, particularly emphasizing that all technical features must be realized within a single product for direct infringement. The Court maintained strict procedural compliance standards, enforcing rigorous deadlines for appeals and evidence submission while limiting the scope of revocation reviews to substantiated arguments. Concurrently, the Administrative Committee approved the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre procedural rules, and its case management system went live. actions_en: Review patent portfolios and freedom-to-operate analyses for hardware components sold in Europe, ensuring functional terms do not inadvertently create direct infringement liability...
Implement dual deadline verification protocols for all UPC filings and immediately express written licensing willingness upon receiving infringement notices to preserve FRAND defen...

Track PMAC caseload volumes, mediation success rates, and fee structures to assess its practical efficacy as a mainstream alternative to court litigation.

topics.headline_zh: 欧洲SEP：英国定费率，UPC强禁令
topics.headline_en: UK Sets Global FRAND Rates; UPC Enforces Injunctions and Anti-Suit Measures
topics.summary_zh: 2025-2026年欧洲SEP格局剧变。英国法院确立全球FRAND费率裁定权并倾向仲裁，成为定价中心；UPC强化禁令执行与反禁诉令，打击NPE及拖延策略。监管层面，欧盟修订TTBER明确许可合规，UKIPO推动透明度改革。中企面临高额交叉许可费与快速诉讼双重压力，需重构全球许可战略以应对管辖权冲突。
key_points: 英国司法主导定价：高院裁定三星-中兴3.92亿美元全球交叉许可费率，上诉法院确认有效要约可中止诉讼，确立英国为全球FRAND核心裁判地。
UPC执法趋严：曼海姆等分院积极签发禁令及反禁诉令（AASI），拒绝中国临时许可干扰，并鼓励PMAC调解，压缩实施者谈判窗口。
监管与合规升级：欧盟新TTBER生效细化专利池与集体谈判反垄断规则；UKIPO推进SEP透明度改革，NPE利用UPC漏洞发起攻势风险加剧。
actions: 建立“英国+UPC”双轨应诉机制：在英国积极主张可比协议与非FRAND商业因素以压低费率，在UPC避免申请临时许可以防触发AASI。
前置许可谈判与证据固化：收到侵权通知须立即书面表达许可意愿，保留抗辩权；审查现有协议以符合新TTBER反垄断合规要求。
评估加入专利池或仲裁条款：关注Sisvel/Via等主流池动态，在合同中预设仲裁条款以规避英国法院的强制费率裁定风险。
watchlist: 英国最高法院对特斯拉/InterDigital案的裁决，及其对全球费率管辖权的最终界定。
UKIPO关于SEP必要性审查与透明度改革的最终政策细则落地时间。
UPC上诉法院关于初步禁令中专利修改及员工保密隔离程序的进一步判例。

sep_ai_analysis.summary_zh: SEP 长窗从 2026.03 累计到现在，共识别 95 条高相关信号。近120天里，许可动作占比最高，欧洲层面仍是最活跃场域。
sep_ai_analysis.summary_en: The SEP long window now spans from Mar 2026 to the present with 95 high-relevance signals. In the recent 120-day window, Licensing lead the mix and Europe-wide remains the busiest forum.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 许可动作已经从背景变量变成当前主线，说明专利池扩围、费率博弈和双边谈判正在持续抬高经营准备门槛。
insight_cards.insight_en: Licensing has moved from a background factor to the main storyline, showing pools, rate-setting, and bilateral talks are raising the preparation bar.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先把许可覆盖、费率对标和 willing licensee 证据准备前置。
insight_cards.action_en: Front-load licence coverage checks, rate benchmarking, and willing-licensee evidence.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 近120天 许可动作 61 / 前120天 0 / 长窗累计 61

insight_cards.insight_zh: 欧洲层面是近120天最活跃的场域，英国紧随其后，争议正在形成多法域联动。
insight_cards.insight_en: Europe-wide is the busiest forum in the recent window, with United Kingdom close behind, pointing to multi-forum spillover.
insight_cards.action_zh: 优先盯住 欧洲层面 的裁判与程序变化，并同步观察外溢到其他法域的连锁影响。
insight_cards.action_en: Track procedural and decisional shifts in Europe-wide first, then watch for spillover into other venues.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 欧洲层面 55 条；英国 23 条

insight_cards.insight_zh: 近120天中企相关信号共有 31 条，占近期样本 33%。重点企业包括 ZTE / Huawei / Oppo。
insight_cards.insight_en: There are 31 China-related signals in the recent 120-day window, or 33% of the sample. Focus companies include ZTE / Huawei / Oppo.
insight_cards.action_zh: 把许可准备、诉讼预案和客户交付协同放到同一张经营清单里，不要只按单案应对。
insight_cards.action_en: Put licensing readiness, litigation planning, and customer-delivery coordination on the same operating checklist rather than treating them as one-off disputes.

insight_cards.insight_zh: 当前约 100% 的专题样本基于全文或公开预览分析，摘要级样本主要用于补趋势，不宜单独放大解读。
insight_cards.insight_en: About 100% of the topic rests on full-text or public-preview analysis; summary-only items are mainly useful for directional trend support.
insight_cards.action_zh: 对涉及重大经营判断的节点，优先回到原文或公开预览核实，再决定是否升级为重点预警。
insight_cards.action_en: For business-critical signals, step back to the source text or public preview before escalating them into priority warnings.
insight_cards.evidence_zh: 全文 29 / 预览 66 / 摘要 0

trend_judgments: 近120天 SEP标准必要专利信号较前120天增加 95 条，整体仍在升温。
当前最强主线仍是许可动作，近120天共 61 条，说明专利池、费率与双边谈判继续主导节奏。
欧洲层面是近120天最活跃的主导法域/场域，共出现 55 条相关信号。
中企相关信号近120天共有 31 条，占近期样本 33%；当前专题中约 100% 的样本基于全文或公开预览分析。
trend_judgments_en: SEP signals are up by 95 over the prior 120 days, pointing to a still-heating window.
Licensing remains the strongest line with 61 recent signals, keeping pools, rates, and bilateral talks at the center.
Europe-wide is the most active forum in the recent 120-day window with 55 signals.

topics.china_impact_summary_zh: 这条长窗显示，SEP 风险已经从传统通信终端延伸到车载与 IoT，并在德国、英国和 UPC 三条诉讼线同步抬升。对中国企业来说，下一阶段重点不只是单案应对，而是把许可准备、诉讼预案和规则跟踪并行推进。
china_action_points: 涉及车载、蜂窝、Wi-Fi 或 IoT 出海业务的中国企业，应尽快核查专利池与双边许可覆盖，避免产品上市后再被动补谈。
在德国、英国和 UPC 有销售、供应链或客户落点的企业，应同步准备 FRAND 谈判材料、可比许可证据与禁令应对预案。
法务、标准与商务团队需要持续跟踪英欧 SEP 政策和竞争法变化，避免沿用单一市场的许可策略应对欧洲。

topics.summary_en: The European Standard-Essential Patent (SEP) landscape is undergoing significant transformation in 2025-2026. UK courts have established themselves as primary venues for determining global Fair, Reasonable, and Non-Discriminatory (FRAND) rates, while the Unified Patent Court (UPC) strengthens enforcement through injunctions and anti-anti-suit injunctions (AASI). This judicial shift pressures implementers to navigate complex jurisdictional conflicts and aggressive non-practicing entity strategies. Concurrently, regulatory frameworks are evolving, with the EU updating competition rules for patent pools and the UK Intellectual Property Office advancing transparency reforms. Companies must adapt their global licensing strategies to address heightened litigation risks, strict compliance requirements, and the increasing likelihood of rapid injunctive relief across key European jurisdictions.
key_points_en: UK courts now serve as central arbiters for global FRAND cross-licensing rates, setting precedents that adjust royalties based on litigation capacity and comparable agreements.
The UPC actively issues injunctions and AASIs against foreign interim licenses, compressing negotiation windows and limiting implementer procedural delays.
Regulatory updates, including revised EU competition guidelines and UK transparency initiatives, increase compliance burdens and expose entities to antitrust scrutiny.
actions_en: Implement a dual-track defense strategy: challenge rate benchmarks in the UK while avoiding foreign interim license applications that may trigger UPC AASIs.
Immediately document licensing willingness upon receiving infringement notices and review existing agreements to ensure alignment with new antitrust compliance standards.
Evaluate participation in major patent pools or contractual arbitration clauses to mitigate exposure to mandatory court-determined global royalty rates.
watchlist_en: Supreme Court rulings clarifying the final scope of global FRAND jurisdiction and rate-setting authority.
Finalization and implementation timelines for UKIPO policies on SEP essentiality reviews and licensing transparency.
UPC appellate decisions regarding patent amendments during preliminary injunctions and employee confidentiality barriers.

topics.headline_zh: 欧盟商标司法收紧与融资新政
topics.headline_en: EU Trademark Law Tightens While New IP Financing Policies Emerge
topics.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，欧盟商标司法趋严：CJEU明确异议权利需全程有效，普通法院强化声誉评估，动态商标因技术功能被拒。同时，德国恶意抢注激增，UKIPO警示假冒风险。政策面利好中小企业，EUIPO基金降低注册成本，新报告拟释放5800亿欧元IP融资潜力，助力企业合规扩张与资产变现。
key_points: 司法裁判趋严：CJEU裁定异议在先权利须在决定时持续有效；普通法院强调全局评估商标声誉联系；纯技术功能动态商标缺乏显著性将被驳回。
合规风险升级：德国AI辅助恶意抢注激增，无使用意图注册可被无效；UKIPO警示假冒耗材关联有组织犯罪，供应链溯源责任加重。
政策红利释放：EUIPO 2026中小企业基金补贴申请费用；新报告建议打通IP融资渠道，拟释放5800亿欧元创新资本，简化地理标志转化程序。
actions: 建立权利动态监控机制，确保异议期间在先商标持续有效及续展合规，规避权利灭失导致败诉风险。
利用EUIPO SME Fund降低欧盟商标注册成本，同步加强德国等核心市场监控以防范AI恶意抢注，完善供应链防伪溯源体系。
规范知识产权资产管理，评估IP质押融资可行性，关注欧盟储蓄与投资联盟配套政策，拓宽创新资金渠道。
watchlist: 欧洲层面是否就“声誉评估”及“技术功能动态商标”裁判口径出台更明确的审查指南或配套安排。
欧盟关于手工艺地理标志（如利摩日瓷器）简化转化程序的后续推广范围及具体实施细则。
EUIPO IP融资政策在欧盟层面的落地进度及中小企业通过IP资产获取信贷的具体操作路径。
topics.summary_en: During the first half of 2026, EU trademark jurisprudence has tightened significantly. The Court of Justice ruled that earlier rights must remain valid throughout opposition proceedings, while the General Court emphasized comprehensive reputation assessments and rejected motion marks based solely on technical functionality. Concurrently, compliance risks have escalated due to a surge in AI-assisted bad-faith filings in Germany and warnings from the UKIPO regarding counterfeit supply chains linked to organized crime. On the policy front, initiatives favor small and medium-sized enterprises. The EUIPO SME Fund reduces registration costs, and new reports propose unlocking substantial innovation capital through improved intellectual property financing mechanisms. These developments collectively support compliant expansion and asset monetization for businesses operating within the European ma...
key_points_en: Judicial standards are tightening: The CJEU mandates that earlier rights must remain valid at the time of the EUIPO decision; the General Court requires global assessment of tradem...
Compliance risks are escalating: Germany reports a surge in AI-assisted bad-faith filings targeting unregistered signs, while the UKIPO warns that counterfeit consumables are linke...
actions_en: Establish dynamic rights monitoring mechanisms to ensure earlier trademarks remain valid and renewed during opposition periods, mitigating the risk of dismissal due to rights lapse...
Utilize the EUIPO SME Fund to lower EU registration costs, while strengthening monitoring in key markets like Germany to prevent AI-driven bad-faith squatting and enhancing supply...
Standardize intellectual property asset management to evaluate the feasibility of IP-backed financing, aligning with EU savings and investment policies to diversify funding sources...
watchlist_en: Whether European authorities will issue clearer examination guidelines or supporting arrangements regarding 'reputation assessment' and 'technical functionality' for motion marks.
The scope of subsequent promotion and specific implementation details for the simplified conversion procedure for artisanal geographical indications across the EU.
The progress of implementing EUIPO IP financing policies at the EU level and the specific operational pathways for SMEs to secure credit using IP assets.

topics.headline_en: UKIPO Warns of Serious Risks from Counterfeit Toner and Ink Cartridges
topics.summary_zh: 英国知识产权局警示假冒碳粉危害，指出全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元且常关联有组织犯罪。造假者往往免责且不赔偿，导致品牌方损失严重。建议出口企业建立合规溯源体系以防范供应链指控，进口商需加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材，强化防伪保护机制。

全球假冒贸易额高达4670亿美元，常关联有组织犯罪网络
需强化防伪保护，建立合规溯源体系与供应商尽调机制

加强进口供应商尽职调查，严禁采购不明来源低价耗材

跟踪欧盟及英国对跨境电商平台假货责任的立法进展
topics.topic_name_zh: 假冒执法 / 品牌保护
topics.summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued a warning regarding the significant harms caused by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly affecting small businesses. The blog highlights that while often perceived as low-risk, fake print consumables can cause severe operational damage. Global trade in counterfeit goods is estimated at USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks. Counterfeit manufacturers typically bear no liability or compensation responsibility for damages incurred by users, leading to substantial financial losses for legitimate brand owners and end-users. To mitigate these risks, businesses are advised to implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms. Establishing robust IP compliance and traceability systems is essential to prevent inadvertent involvement in counterfeit...
key_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.
Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.
Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms.
actions_en: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.
Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.
Upgrade anti-counterfeiting technical measures and enhance brand protection with continuous infringement monitoring mechanisms.
watchlist_en: Monitor subsequent enforcement dynamics from UKIPO and EUIPO regarding counterfeit consumables.
Track changes in global counterfeit trade data and new trends in organized criminal activities.
Follow legislative progress in the EU and UK concerning the liability of cross-border e-commerce platforms for counterfeit goods.
